---- MODULE Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus ----
EXTENDS Integers, NaturalsInduction, TLAPS
(* ---- Content from module Sets ---- *)
  \** NB: Module NaturalsInduction comes from the TLAPS library, usually
  \** installed in /usr/local/lib/tlaps. Make sure this is in your Toolbox
  \** search path, see Preferences/TLA+ Preferences.

IsBijection(f, S, T) == /\ f \in [S -> T]
                        /\ \A x, y \in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
                        /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : f[x] = y


IsFiniteSet(S) == \E n \in Nat : \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

(****************************************************************************)
(* Finite sets and cardinality are defined in the TLA+ standard module      *)
(* FiniteSets, but this is not yet natively supported by TLAPS. For the     *)
(* time being, we use the following axiom for defining set cardinality.     *)
(****************************************************************************)
\* Cardinality(S) == CHOOSE n : (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

CONSTANT Cardinality(_)
AXIOM CardinalityAxiom ==
         \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) =>
           \A n : (n = Cardinality(S)) <=>
                    (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityInNat == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => Cardinality(S) \in Nat
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityZero ==
           /\ IsFiniteSet({})
           /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
           /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ (Cardinality(S)=0) => (S = {})
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityPlusOne ==
    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
           NEW x, x \notin S
    PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
           /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) = Cardinality(S) + 1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOne == \A m : /\ IsFiniteSet({m})
                                 /\ Cardinality({m}) = 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityTwo == \A m, p : m # p => 
                              /\ IsFiniteSet({m,p})
                              /\ Cardinality({m,p}) = 2
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==  
  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat 
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==
  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, 
         IsBijection(f, S, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IsBijectionTransitive ==
  ASSUME NEW f1, NEW f2, NEW S, NEW T, NEW U, 
           IsBijection(f1, S, U),
           IsBijection(f2, U, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, S, T)
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IsBijectionCardinality ==
  \A f, S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
               /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
               => (IsBijection(f, S, T) <=> Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T))

LEMMA CardinalitySetMinus ==
      ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
             NEW x \in S
      PROVE /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
            /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
PROOF OBVIOUS

========================================

item.text: The useful route is proving `S = (S \ {x}) \cup {x}` and deriving finiteness/cardinality through the admitted plus-one lemma, but I first need TLAPS to accept the set algebra.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cp Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla /tmp/Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.orig.tla
apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla
@@
 LEMMA CardinalitySetMinus ==
       ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
              NEW x \\in S
       PROVE /\\ IsFiniteSet(S \\ {x})
             /\\ Cardinality(S \\ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  <1>1. x \\notin S \\ {x} BY DEF \\notin
+  <1>2. S = (S \\ {x}) \\cup {x} BY <1>1
+  <1>3. SUFFICES ASSUME IsFiniteSet(S \\ {x})
+                 PROVE  Cardinality(S \\ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
+    OBVIOUS
+  <1>4. QED BY CardinalityPlusOne, <1>1, <1>2, <1>3
 
 ========================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla"

item.text: `\\notin` isn’t a definable operator for TLAPS here, so I’ll rewrite that step using plain negated membership.

item.command:

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla
@@
 PROOF
-  <1>1. x \\notin S \\ {x} BY DEF \\notin
+  <1>1. ~(x \\in S \\ {x}) OBVIOUS
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla"

------------------------- MODULE NaturalsInduction -------------------------- (***************************************************************************) (* This module contains useful theorems for inductive proofs and recursive *) (* definitions over the naturals. *) (* *) (* Some of the statements of the theorems are decomposed in terms of *) (* definitions. This is done for two reasons: *) (* *) (* - It makes it easier for the backends to instantiate the theorems *) (* when those definitions are not expanded. *) (* *) (* - It can be convenient when writing proofs to use those definitions *) (* rather than having to write out their expansions. *) (***************************************************************************) EXTENDS Integers (***************************************************************************) (* The following is the simple statement of inductions over the naturals. *) (* For predicates P defined by a moderately complex operator, it is often *) (* useful to hide the operator definition before using this theorem. That *) (* is, you first define a suitable operator P (not necessarily by that *) (* name), prove the two hypotheses of the theorem, and then hide the *) (* definition of P when using the theorem. *) (***************************************************************************) THEOREM NatInduction == ASSUME NEW P(_), P(0), \A n \in Nat : P(n) => P(n+1) PROVE \A n \in Nat : P(n) (***************************************************************************) (* A useful corollary of NatInduction *) (***************************************************************************) THEOREM DownwardNatInduction == ASSUME NEW P(_), NEW m \in Nat, P(m), \A n \in 1 .. m : P(n) => P(n-1) PROVE P(0) (***************************************************************************) (* The following theorem expresses a stronger induction principle, *) (* also known as course-of-values induction, where the induction *) (* hypothesis is available for all strictly smaller natural numbers. *) (***************************************************************************) THEOREM GeneralNatInduction == ASSUME NEW P(_), \A n \in Nat : (\A m \in 0..(n-1) : P(m)) => P(n) PROVE \A n \in Nat : P(n) (***************************************************************************) (* The following theorem expresses the ``least-number principle'': *) (* if P(n) is true for some natural number n then there is a *) (* smallest natural number for which P is true. It could be derived in *) (* module WellFoundedInduction as a corollary of the fact that the natural *) (* numbers are well ordered, but we give a direct proof. *) (***************************************************************************) THEOREM SmallestNatural == ASSUME NEW P(_), NEW n \in Nat, P(n) PROVE \E m \in Nat : /\ P(m) /\ \A k \in 0 .. m-1 : ~ P(k) (***************************************************************************) (* The following theorem says that a recursively defined function f over *) (* the natural numbers is well-defined if for every n \in Nat the *) (* definition of f[n] depends only on arguments smaller than n. *) (***************************************************************************) THEOREM RecursiveFcnOfNat == ASSUME NEW Def(_,_), ASSUME NEW n \in Nat, NEW g, NEW h, \A i \in 0..(n-1) : g[i] = h[i] PROVE Def(g, n) = Def(h, n) PROVE LET f[n \in Nat] == Def(f, n) IN f = [n \in Nat |-> Def(f, n)] (***************************************************************************) (* The following theorem NatInductiveDef is what you use to justify a *) (* function defined by primitive recursion over the naturals. *) (***************************************************************************) NatInductiveDefHypothesis(f, f0, Def(_,_)) == (f = CHOOSE g : g = [i \in Nat |-> IF i = 0 THEN f0 ELSE Def(g[i-1], i)]) NatInductiveDefConclusion(f, f0, Def(_,_)) == f = [i \in Nat |-> IF i = 0 THEN f0 ELSE Def(f[i-1], i)] THEOREM NatInductiveDef == ASSUME NEW Def(_,_), NEW f, NEW f0, NatInductiveDefHypothesis(f, f0, Def) PROVE NatInductiveDefConclusion(f, f0, Def) (***************************************************************************) (* The following two theorems allow you to prove the type of a recursively *) (* defined function over the natural numbers. *) (***************************************************************************) THEOREM RecursiveFcnOfNatType == ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW Def(_,_), f = [n \in Nat |-> Def(f,n)], ASSUME NEW n \in Nat, NEW g, \A i \in 0 .. n-1 : g[i] \in S PROVE Def(g,n) \in S PROVE f \in [Nat -> S] THEOREM NatInductiveDefType == Def(g,n)], ASSUME NEW n \in Nat, NEW ff, NEW gg, \A i \in 0..(n-1) : ff[i] = gg[i] PROVE Def(ff, n) = Def(gg, n) PROVE f = g THEOREM NatInductiveUnique == ASSUME NEW Def(_,_), NEW f, NEW g, NEW f0, item.text: I’ll test how much Isabelle can prove if we explicitly unfold the finite-set/bijection definitions, then refine from the smallest failing subgoal.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla
@@
 PROOF
-  <1>1. ~(x \\in S \\ {x}) OBVIOUS
-  <1>2. S = (S \\ {x}) \\cup {x} BY <1>1
-  <1>3. SUFFICES ASSUME IsFiniteSet(S \\ {x})
-                 PROVE  Cardinality(S \\ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
-    OBVIOUS
-  <1>4. QED BY CardinalityPlusOne, <1>1, <1>2, <1>3
+  BY DEF IsFiniteSet, IsBijection
 
 ========================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla"

item.text: I’ll switch from the backward plus-one attempt to a constructive proof from the finite-set witness.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla
@@
 PROOF
-  BY DEF IsFiniteSet, IsBijection
+  <1>1. Cardinality(S) \\in Nat BY CardinalityInNat
+  <1>2. Cardinality(S) # 0
+    PROOF
+      <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME Cardinality(S) = 0 PROVE FALSE OBVIOUS
+      <2>2. S = {} BY <2>1, CardinalityZero
+      <2>3. QED BY <2>2, ASSUME
+    QED
+  <1>3. LET n == Cardinality(S) IN n \\in Nat /\\ n # 0 /\\ \\E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
+    BY <1>1, <1>2, CardinalityAxiom
+  <1>4. PICK f : IsBijection(f, 1..Cardinality(S), S)
+    BY <1>3
+  <1>5. \\E k \\in 1..Cardinality(S) : f[k] = x
+    BY <1>4, ASSUME DEF IsBijection
+  <1>6. PICK k \\in 1..Cardinality(S) : f[k] = x
+    BY <1>5
+  DEFINE g == [i \\in 1..(Cardinality(S)-1) |-> IF i < k THEN f[i] ELSE f[i+1]]
+  <1>7. IsBijection(g, 1..(Cardinality(S)-1), S \\ {x})
+    BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>4, <1>6 DEF g, IsBijection
+  <1>8. Cardinality(S)-1 \\in Nat /\\ \\E g : IsBijection(g, 1..(Cardinality(S)-1), S \\ {x})
+    BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>7
+  <1>9. IsFiniteSet(S \\ {x})
+    BY <1>8 DEF IsFiniteSet
+  <1>10. Cardinality(S \\ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
+    BY <1>9, <1>8, CardinalityAxiom
+  <1>11. QED BY <1>9, <1>10
 
 ========================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla"

item.text: The remaining parse issue is in the contradiction subproof, so I’ll remove the problematic `SUFFICES` idiom there.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla
@@
   <1>2. Cardinality(S) # 0
     PROOF
-      <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME Cardinality(S) = 0 PROVE FALSE OBVIOUS
-      <2>2. S = {} BY <2>1, CardinalityZero
-      <2>3. QED BY <2>2, ASSUME
+      <2>1. ASSUME Cardinality(S) = 0
+      <2>2. S = {} BY <2>1, CardinalityZero
+      <2>3. FALSE BY <2>2, ASSUME
+      <2>4. QED BY <2>3
     QED
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla"

item.text: I’ll use direct contradiction reasoning for nonzero cardinality and keep the larger constructive proof for TLAPS to check.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla
@@
-  <1>2. Cardinality(S) # 0
-    PROOF
-      <2>1. ASSUME Cardinality(S) = 0
-      <2>2. S = {} BY <2>1, CardinalityZero
-      <2>3. FALSE BY <2>2, ASSUME
-      <2>4. QED BY <2>3
-    QED
+  <1>2. Cardinality(S) # 0 BY ASSUME, CardinalityZero
*** End Patch
PATCH

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('LEMMA CardinalitySetMinus'))
end=s.index('\\n\\n========================================', start)
proof='''PROOF
  <1>1. Cardinality(S) \\\\in Nat BY CardinalityInNat
  <1>2. \\\\E f : IsBijection(f, 1..Cardinality(S), S)
    BY <1>1, CardinalityAxiom
  <1>3. PICK f : IsBijection(f, 1..Cardinality(S), S) BY <1>2
  <1>4. \\\\E k \\\\in 1..Cardinality(S) : f[k] = x BY <1>3 DEF IsBijection
  <1>5. PICK k \\\\in 1..Cardinality(S) : f[k] = x BY <1>4
  <1>6. Cardinality(S) # 0 BY <1>5
  <1>. DEFINE n == Cardinality(S)
              g == [i \\\\in 1..(n-1) |-> IF i < k THEN f[i] ELSE f[i+1]]
  <1>7. IsBijection(g, 1..(n-1), S \\\\ {x})
    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6 DEF IsBijection, n, g
  <1>8. IsFiniteSet(S \\\\ {x}) BY <1>1, <1>7 DEF IsFiniteSet, n
  <1>9. Cardinality(S \\\\ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
    BY <1>1, <1>7, <1>8, CardinalityAxiom
  <1> QED BY <1>8, <1>9'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
nl -ba Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla | sed -n '91,120p'
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla"

item.text: The automated tactics still miss the interval shifting argument, so I’ll write the three bijection conjuncts explicitly with case splits.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla')
s=p.read_text()
old='''  <1>7. IsBijection(g, 1..(n-1), S \\\\ {x})
    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, IsaM(\"auto\") DEF IsBijection, n, g'''
new='''  <1>7. g \\\\in [1..(n-1) -> S \\\\ {x}]
    PROOF
      <2>1. \\\\A i \\\\in 1..(n-1) : g[i] \\\\in S \\\\ {x}
        PROOF
          <3>1. TAKE i \\\\in 1..(n-1)
          <3>2. CASE i < k
            <4>1. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i # k BY <1>5, <3>1, <3>2 DEF n
            <4>2. f[i] \\\\in S /\\\\ f[i] # x BY <1>3, <1>5, <4>1 DEF IsBijection
            <4>3. g[i] = f[i] BY <3>1, <3>2 DEF g
            <4>. QED BY <4>2, <4>3
          <3>3. CASE ~(i < k)
            <4>1. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i+1 # k BY <1>5, <3>1, <3>3 DEF n
            <4>2. f[i+1] \\\\in S /\\\\ f[i+1] # x BY <1>3, <1>5, <4>1 DEF IsBijection
            <4>3. g[i] = f[i+1] BY <3>1, <3>3 DEF g
            <4>. QED BY <4>2, <4>3
          <3>. QED BY <3>2, <3>3
        QED
      <2>. QED BY <2>1 DEF g
    QED
  <1>8. \\\\A i, j \\\\in 1..(n-1) : (i # j) => (g[i] # g[j])
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE i \\\\in 1..(n-1), j \\\\in 1..(n-1)
      <2>2. ASSUME i # j
      <2>3. CASE i < k /\\\\ j < k
        <3>1. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ j \\\\in 1..n BY <2>1, <2>3 DEF n
        <3>2. g[i] = f[i] /\\\\ g[j] = f[j] BY <2>1, <2>3 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <1>3, <2>2, <3>1, <3>2 DEF IsBijection
      <2>4. CASE i < k /\\\\ ~(j < k)
        <3>1. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ j+1 \\\\in 1..n BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>4 DEF n
        <3>2. i # j+1 BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>4 DEF n
        <3>3. g[i] = f[i] /\\\\ g[j] = f[j+1] BY <2>1, <2>4 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <1>3, <3>1, <3>2, <3>3 DEF IsBijection
      <2>5. CASE ~(i < k) /\\\\ j < k
        <3>1. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ j \\\\in 1..n BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>5 DEF n
        <3>2. i+1 # j BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>5 DEF n
        <3>3. g[i] = f[i+1] /\\\\ g[j] = f[j] BY <2>1, <2>5 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <1>3, <3>1, <3>2, <3>3 DEF IsBijection
      <2>6. CASE ~(i < k) /\\\\ ~(j < k)
        <3>1. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ j+1 \\\\in 1..n BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>6 DEF n
        <3>2. i+1 # j+1 BY <2>2
        <3>3. g[i] = f[i+1] /\\\\ g[j] = f[j+1] BY <2>1, <2>6 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <1>3, <3>1, <3>2, <3>3 DEF IsBijection
      <2>. QED BY <2>3, <2>4, <2>5, <2>6
    QED
  <1>9. \\\\A y \\\\in S \\\\ {x} : \\\\E i \\\\in 1..(n-1) : g[i] = y
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE y \\\\in S \\\\ {x}
      <2>2. y \\\\in S /\\\\ y # x BY <2>1
      <2>3. \\\\E j \\\\in 1..n : f[j] = y BY <1>3, <2>2 DEF IsBijection
      <2>4. PICK j \\\\in 1..n : f[j] = y BY <2>3
      <2>5. j # k BY <1>5, <2>2, <2>4
      <2>6. CASE j < k
        <3>1. j \\\\in 1..(n-1) BY <1>5, <2>4, <2>6 DEF n
        <3>2. g[j] = y BY <2>4, <2>6, <3>1 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <3>1, <3>2
      <2>7. CASE ~(j < k)
        <3>1. j-1 \\\\in 1..(n-1) BY <1>5, <2>4, <2>5, <2>7 DEF n
        <3>2. ~(j-1 < k) BY <2>5, <2>7
        <3>3. g[j-1] = y BY <2>4, <3>1, <3>2 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <3>1, <3>3
      <2>. QED BY <2>6, <2>7
    QED
  <1>10. IsBijection(g, 1..(n-1), S \\\\ {x})
    BY <1>7, <1>8, <1>9 DEF IsBijection'''
if old not in s:
    raise SystemExit('old not found')
s=s.replace(old,new)

from pathlib import Path

new='''  <1>7. g \\\\in [1..(n-1) -> S \\\\ {x}]
    PROOF
      <2>1. \\\\A i \\\\in 1..(n-1) : g[i] \\\\in S \\\\ {x}
        PROOF
          <3>1. TAKE i \\\\in 1..(n-1)
          <3>2. CASE i < k
            <4>1. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i # k BY <1>5, <3>1, <3>2 DEF n
            <4>2. f[i] \\\\in S /\\\\ f[i] # x BY <1>3, <1>5, <4>1 DEF IsBijection
            <4>3. g[i] = f[i] BY <3>1, <3>2 DEF g
            <4>. QED BY <4>2, <4>3
          <3>3. CASE ~(i < k)
            <4>1. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i+1 # k BY <1>5, <3>1, <3>3 DEF n
            <4>2. f[i+1] \\\\in S /\\\\ f[i+1] # x BY <1>3, <1>5, <4>1 DEF IsBijection
            <4>3. g[i] = f[i+1] BY <3>1, <3>3 DEF g
            <4>. QED BY <4>2, <4>3
          <3>. QED BY <3>2, <3>3
        QED
      <2>. QED BY <2>1 DEF g
    QED
  <1>8. \\\\A i, j \\\\in 1..(n-1) : (i # j) => (g[i] # g[j])
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE i \\\\in 1..(n-1), j \\\\in 1..(n-1)
      <2>2. ASSUME i # j
      <2>3. CASE i < k /\\\\ j < k
        <3>1. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ j \\\\in 1..n BY <2>1, <2>3 DEF n
        <3>2. g[i] = f[i] /\\\\ g[j] = f[j] BY <2>1, <2>3 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <1>3, <2>2, <3>1, <3>2 DEF IsBijection
      <2>4. CASE i < k /\\\\ ~(j < k)
        <3>1. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ j+1 \\\\in 1..n BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>4 DEF n
        <3>2. i # j+1 BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>4 DEF n
        <3>3. g[i] = f[i] /\\\\ g[j] = f[j+1] BY <2>1, <2>4 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <1>3, <3>1, <3>2, <3>3 DEF IsBijection
      <2>5. CASE ~(i < k) /\\\\ j < k
        <3>1. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ j \\\\in 1..n BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>5 DEF n
        <3>2. i+1 # j BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>5 DEF n
        <3>3. g[i] = f[i+1] /\\\\ g[j] = f[j] BY <2>1, <2>5 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <1>3, <3>1, <3>2, <3>3 DEF IsBijection
      <2>6. CASE ~(i < k) /\\\\ ~(j < k)
        <3>1. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ j+1 \\\\in 1..n BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>6 DEF n
        <3>2. i+1 # j+1 BY <2>2
        <3>3. g[i] = f[i+1] /\\\\ g[j] = f[j+1] BY <2>1, <2>6 DEF g
        <3>. QED BY <1>3, <3>1, <3>2, <3>3 DEF IsBijection
      <2>. QED BY <2>3, <2>4, <2>5, <2>6
    QED
  <1>9. \\\\A y \\\\in S \\\\ {x} : \\\\E i \\\\in 1..(n-1) : g[i] = y
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE y \\\\in S \\\\ {x}

      <2>3. \\\\E j \\\\in 1..n : f[j] = y BY <1>3, <2>2 DEF IsBijection

item.text: The parser still dislikes anonymous subcase closures; I’ll number every remaining anonymous `QED` step.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla')
s=p.read_text()
# simple line-by-line number anonymous QEDs based on previous sibling number + 1 within same indentation/level
lines=s.splitlines()
last_num_by_level={}
out=[]
import re
for line in lines:
    m=re.match(r'(\\s*)<(\\d+)>\\. QED(.*)', line)
    if m:
        indent, level, rest=m.groups(); level=int(level)
        num=last_num_by_level.get(level,0)+1
        line=f'{indent}<{level}>{num}. QED{rest}'
        last_num_by_level[level]=num
        out.append(line); continue
    m=re.match(r'\\s*<(\\d+)>(\\d+)\\.', line)
    if m:
        level=int(m.group(1)); num=int(m.group(2)); last_num_by_level[level]=num
        # reset deeper
        for k in list(last_num_by_level):
            if k>level: del last_num_by_level[k]
    out.append(line)
p.write_text('\\n'.join(out)+'\\n')
PY
rg \"<\\d+>\\. QED\" -n Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla || true

item.text: The hand expansion is getting parser-heavy, so I’ll try proving each bijection conjunct separately with SMT instead of one monolithic goal.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('  <1>7. g \\\\in', s.index('DEFINE n'))
end=s.index('  <1>11. IsFiniteSet', start)
block='''  <1>7. g \\\\in [1..(n-1) -> S \\\\ {x}]
    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g
  <1>8. \\\\A i, j \\\\in 1..(n-1) : (i # j) => (g[i] # g[j])
    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g
  <1>9. \\\\A y \\\\in S \\\\ {x} : \\\\E i \\\\in 1..(n-1) : g[i] = y
    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g
  <1>10. IsBijection(g, 1..(n-1), S \\\\ {x})
    BY <1>7, <1>8, <1>9 DEF IsBijection
'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+block+s[end:])
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla"

  <1>8. \\\\A i, j \\\\in 1..(n-1) : (i # j) => (g[i] # g[j])
    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g
  <1>9. \\\\A y \\\\in S \\\\ {x} : \\\\E i \\\\in 1..(n-1) : g[i] = y
    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g
  <1>10. IsBijection(g, 1..(n-1), S \\\\ {x})
    BY <1>7, <1>8, <1>9 DEF IsBijection
'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+block+s[end:])

item.text: The split still needs explicit witnesses/case facts. I’ll use TLAPS’s stronger bundled prover pragmas before further hand-splitting.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "for pragma in 'CVC4' 'Yices' 'veriT' 'Z3' 'AllSMT' 'SMTT(30)' 'CVC4T(30)' 'IsaM(\"force\")' 'IsaM(\"blast\")' 'IsaWithSetExtensionality'; do
  cp Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla /tmp/testproof.tla
  python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('/tmp/testproof.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g', '"'$pragma DEF IsBijection, n, g'"')
p.write_text(s)
PY
  echo \"--- "'$pragma ---"
  ~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/testproof.tla >/tmp/tlapout 2>&1
  code=$?
  tail -40 /tmp/tlapout
  echo code=$code
  [ $code -eq 0 ] && break
done'

/bin/zsh -lc "for pragma in 'CVC4' 'Yices' 'veriT' 'Z3' 'AllSMT' 'SMTT(30)' 'CVC4T(30)' 'IsaM(\"force\")' 'IsaM(\"blast\")' 'IsaWithSetExtensionality'; do

  [ $code -eq 0 ] && break
done'

item.text: I’ll add named facts extracted from the original bijection; that should make the constructed function proof much easier for backends.

*** Update File: Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla
@@
   <1>. DEFINE n == Cardinality(S)
               g == [i \\in 1..(n-1) |-> IF i < k THEN f[i] ELSE f[i+1]]
-  <1>7. g \\in [1..(n-1) -> S \\ {x}]
-    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g
-  <1>8. \\A i, j \\in 1..(n-1) : (i # j) => (g[i] # g[j])
-    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g
-  <1>9. \\A y \\in S \\ {x} : \\E i \\in 1..(n-1) : g[i] = y
-    BY <1>3, <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF IsBijection, n, g
+  <1>7. f \\in [1..n -> S] BY <1>3 DEF IsBijection, n
+  <1>8. \\A i, j \\in 1..n : (i # j) => (f[i] # f[j])
+    BY <1>3 DEF IsBijection, n

+    BY <1>3 DEF IsBijection, n
+  <1>10. f[k] = x BY <1>5

+    BY <1>5, <1>6, <1>8, SMT DEF n, g

+    BY <1>5, <1>6, <1>8, <1>9, <1>10, SMT DEF n, g

-    BY <1>7, <1>8, <1>9 DEF IsBijection
-  <1>11. IsFiniteSet(S \\ {x}) BY <1>1, <1>10 DEF IsFiniteSet, n

+  <1>15. IsFiniteSet(S \\ {x}) BY <1>1, <1>14 DEF IsFiniteSet, n
+  <1>16. Cardinality(S \\ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
+    BY <1>1, <1>14, <1>15, CardinalityAxiom
+  <1> QED BY <1>15, <1>16
*** End Patch

item.text: I’ll switch to the `SUFFICES ASSUME NEW ... PROVE ...` style used elsewhere in these benchmark proofs.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla')
s=p.read_text()
old='''  <1>11. g \\\\in [1..(n-1) -> S \\\\ {x}]
    BY <1>5, <1>6, <1>7, <1>8, <1>10, SMT DEF n, g'''
new='''  <1>11. g \\\\in [1..(n-1) -> S \\\\ {x}]
    PROOF
      <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW i \\\\in 1..(n-1)
                     PROVE  g[i] \\\\in S \\\\ {x}
        OBVIOUS
      <2>2. CASE i < k
        <3>1. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i # k BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>2 DEF n
        <3>2. f[i] \\\\in S BY <1>7, <3>1
        <3>3. f[i] # x BY <1>8, <1>5, <1>10, <3>1
        <3>4. g[i] = f[i] BY <2>1, <2>2 DEF g
        <3>5. QED BY <3>2, <3>3, <3>4
      <2>3. CASE ~(i < k)
        <3>1. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i+1 # k BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>3 DEF n
        <3>2. f[i+1] \\\\in S BY <1>7, <3>1
        <3>3. f[i+1] # x BY <1>8, <1>5, <1>10, <3>1
        <3>4. g[i] = f[i+1] BY <2>1, <2>3 DEF g
        <3>5. QED BY <3>2, <3>3, <3>4
      <2>4. QED BY <2>2, <2>3
    QED'''
if old not in s: raise SystemExit('old not found')
p.write_text(s.replace(old,new))
PY

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('  <1>11. g \\\\in [1..(n-1) -> S \\\\ {x}]')
end=s.index('  <1>12. \\\\A i, j', start)
new='''  <1>11. \\\\A i \\\\in 1..(n-1) : g[i] \\\\in S \\\\ {x}
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE i \\\\in 1..(n-1)
      <2>2. CASE i < k
        <3>1. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i # k BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>2 DEF n
        <3>2. f[i] \\\\in S BY <1>7, <3>1
        <3>3. f[i] # x BY <1>8, <1>5, <1>10, <3>1
        <3>4. g[i] = f[i] BY <2>1, <2>2 DEF g
        <3>5. QED BY <3>2, <3>3, <3>4
      <2>3. CASE ~(i < k)
        <3>1. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i+1 # k BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>3 DEF n
        <3>2. f[i+1] \\\\in S BY <1>7, <3>1
        <3>3. f[i+1] # x BY <1>8, <1>5, <1>10, <3>1
        <3>4. g[i] = f[i+1] BY <2>1, <2>3 DEF g
        <3>5. QED BY <3>2, <3>3, <3>4
      <2>4. QED BY <2>2, <2>3
    QED
  <1>12. g \\\\in [1..(n-1) -> S \\\\ {x}]
    BY <1>11 DEF g
'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+new+s[end:])
PY

item.text: I’ll quickly isolate the proof syntax issue in a tiny TLA+ file so the final proof doesn’t fight the parser.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat >/tmp/SyntaxTest.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE SyntaxTest ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == \\A i \\in 1..3 : TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. TAKE i \\in 1..3
  <1>2. CASE i < 2
    <2>1. TRUE OBVIOUS
    <2>2. QED BY <2>1
  <1>3. CASE ~(i < 2)
    <2>1. TRUE OBVIOUS
    <2>2. QED BY <2>1
  <1>4. QED BY <1>2, <1>3
QED
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/SyntaxTest.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "cat >/tmp/SyntaxTest.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE SyntaxTest ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == \\A i \\in 1..3 : TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. TAKE i \\in 1..3
  <1>2. CASE i < 2
    <2>1. TRUE OBVIOUS
    <2>2. QED BY <2>1
  <1>3. CASE ~(i < 2)
    <2>1. TRUE OBVIOUS
    <2>2. QED BY <2>1
  <1>4. QED BY <1>2, <1>3
QED
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/SyntaxTest.tla"

item.text: The parser test shows final `QED` syntax is stricter than I assumed; I’ll switch closings to the accepted `<n> QED` form.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat >/tmp/SyntaxTest.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE SyntaxTest ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == \\A i \\in 1..3 : TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. TAKE i \\in 1..3
  <1>2. CASE i < 2
    <2>1. TRUE OBVIOUS
    <2> QED BY <2>1
  <1>3. CASE ~(i < 2)
    <2>1. TRUE OBVIOUS
    <2> QED BY <2>1
  <1> QED BY <1>2, <1>3
QED
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/SyntaxTest.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "cat >/tmp/SyntaxTest.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE SyntaxTest ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == \\A i \\in 1..3 : TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. TAKE i \\in 1..3
  <1>2. CASE i < 2
    <2>1. TRUE OBVIOUS

item.text: I found TLAPS has a subtle parser rule: a subproof ending with a `QED` step can’t also be followed by a bare `QED` in that context. I’ll close subproofs using a final proved assertion instead.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat >/tmp/SyntaxTest4.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE SyntaxTest4 ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. \\A i \\in 1..3 : i \\in 1..3
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE i \\in 1..3
      <2>2. CASE i < 2
        <3>1. i \\in 1..3 BY <2>1
      <2>3. CASE ~(i < 2)
        <3>1. i \\in 1..3 BY <2>1
      <2>4. i \\in 1..3 BY <2>2, <2>3
    QED
  <1>2. QED BY <1>1
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/SyntaxTest4.tla"

      <2>3. CASE ~(i < 2)

      <2>4. i \\in 1..3 BY <2>2, <2>3
    QED
  <1>2. QED BY <1>1
====
EOF

item.text: I’ve pinned down the parser quirk: nested subproofs need a bare closing `QED`, not an internal numbered `QED` step. I’ll rewrite that range subproof accordingly.

PROOF
  <1>1. \\A i \\in 1..3 : i \\in 1..3
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE i \\in 1..3
      <2>2. CASE i < 2
        <3>1. i \\in 1..3 BY <2>1
        <3> QED BY <3>1
      <2>3. CASE ~(i < 2)
        <3>1. i \\in 1..3 BY <2>1
        <3> QED BY <3>1
      <2> QED BY <2>2, <2>3
    QED
  <1>2. QED BY <1>1
====
EOF

item.text: I’ll expand just those two helper facts with simple case splits and witnesses.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Consensus_CardinalitySetMinus.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('''  <1>7. \\\\A i \\\\in U : h(i) \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ h(i) # k
    BY <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF U, h, n''','''  <1>7. \\\\A i \\\\in U : h(i) \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ h(i) # k
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE i \\\\in U
      <2>2. CASE i < k
        <3>1. h(i) = i BY <2>2 DEF h
        <3>2. i \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i # k BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>2 DEF U, n
        <3>3. QED BY <3>1, <3>2
      <2>3. CASE ~(i < k)
        <3>1. h(i) = i+1 BY <2>3 DEF h
        <3>2. i+1 \\\\in 1..n /\\\\ i+1 # k BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>3 DEF U, n
        <3>3. QED BY <3>1, <3>2
      <2>4. QED BY <2>2, <2>3
    QED''')
s=s.replace('''  <1>9. \\\\A j \\\\in 1..n : j # k => \\\\E i \\\\in U : h(i) = j
    BY <1>5, <1>6, SMT DEF U, h, n''','''  <1>9. \\\\A j \\\\in 1..n : j # k => \\\\E i \\\\in U : h(i) = j
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE j \\\\in 1..n
      <2>2. ASSUME j # k
      <2>3. CASE j < k
        <3>1. j \\\\in U BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>3 DEF U, n
        <3>2. h(j) = j BY <2>3 DEF h
        <3>3. QED BY <3>1, <3>2
      <2>4. CASE ~(j < k)
        <3>1. j-1 \\\\in U BY <1>5, <2>1, <2>2, <2>4 DEF U, n
        <3>2. ~(j-1 < k) BY <2>2, <2>4
        <3>3. h(j-1) = j BY <3>2 DEF h

---- MODULE SyntaxTest7 ----

THEOREM T == TRUE
PROOF
  <1>1. \\A i \\in 1..3 : i \\in 1..3
    PROOF
      <2>1. TAKE i \\in 1..3
      <2>2. i \\in 1..3 BY <2>1

====

item.text: I’ll add explicit `n \in Nat` and test a tiny interval-arithmetic obligation to guide the final patch.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat >/tmp/IntervalTest.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE IntervalTest ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == ASSUME NEW n \\in Nat, n # 0, NEW k \\in 1..n, NEW i \\in 1..(n-1), i < k
             PROVE i \\in 1..n /\\ i # k
PROOF
  BY SMT
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/IntervalTest.tla
cat >/tmp/IntervalTest2.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE IntervalTest2 ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == ASSUME NEW n \\in Nat, n # 0, NEW k \\in 1..n, NEW i \\in 1..(n-1), ~(i < k)
             PROVE i+1 \\in 1..n /\\ i+1 # k
PROOF
  BY SMT
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/IntervalTest2.tla
cat >/tmp/IntervalTest3.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE IntervalTest3 ----
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS
THEOREM T == ASSUME NEW n \\in Nat, n # 0, NEW k \\in 1..n, NEW j \\in 1..n, j # k, j < k
             PROVE j \\in 1..(n-1)
PROOF
  BY SMT
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/IntervalTest3.tla
cat >/tmp/IntervalTest4.tla <<'EOF'
